title: daemon/api.rs: no HTTP rate limiting on any endpoint
description: Note: nie-0j6b.7 covers the JMAP + /api/send + /api/wallet/pay angle. This is an extension of that finding: ALL api_router routes (/api/whoami, /api/users, /api/wallet/balance, /jmap/upload, /jmap/download) also have no rate limiting, not just the write endpoints. /api/wallet/balance calls balance_with_confirmations(1) which may scan the wallet DB; /jmap/upload accepts arbitrary binary data bounded only by axum body limits (which are not set). A local process with the token can call /jmap/upload in a tight loop with large bodies, growing jmap.db without bound. The existing issue covers some endpoints; none are actually covered by a rate limit. This is a reminder that the fix must cover ALL routes, not just the ones mentioned in nie-0j6b.7.
